target.title: fix(agent): surface non_deliverable_terminal_turn with specific message and auto-retry
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a model turn ends with `stop_reason: tool_use` but no explicit terminal delivery, the runtime classifies it as `non_deliverable_terminal_turn`. This `terminalError` was only recorded in trajectory metadata and never surfaced to the reply layer. Users saw the generic "Something went wrong… use /new" message, which gives misleading remediation advice and hides the actual failure.

## Why This Change Was Made

Three complementary changes:

1. **Propagate `trajectoryTerminalError`** from attempt trajectory through `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta` so the reply layer can see the terminal error reason
2. **Auto-retry once** — the model produced no output, so retry is idempotent (same pattern as `consumeTransientHttpRetry`)
3. **Surface a specific, actionable message** when retry is exhausted

## User Impact

- Users see an actionable message instead of generic "Something went wrong… use /new"
- Intermittent `non_deliverable_terminal_turn` errors self-recover via auto-retry
- `/new` is no longer the primary remediation for this class of failure

## Files changed

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — add `trajectoryTerminalError` to return
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts` — add type field
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts` — add to `EmbeddedAgentRunMeta`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` — propagate through meta
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-failure-copy.ts` — add user-facing message constant
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` — auto-retry + specific message
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` — 3 new tests

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `non_deliverable_terminal_turn` (stop_reason=tool_use, no delivery) surfaced as generic "Something went wrong… use /new" with no diagnostic signal and no retry.

Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw branch fix/non-deliverable-terminal-turn-message (base commit d1ab308f5c).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `rm -rf dist && pnpm build` — clean build from scratch, exit 0
2. Verified built output contains all new code:
   - `dist/agent-runner-failure-copy-BZSo8SgJ.js`: user-facing message text present
   - `dist/reply-usage-state-D-I1Ghsz.js`: auto-retry logic (nonDeliverableTurnRetriesRemaining, consumeNonDeliverableTurnRetry, "Non-deliverable terminal turn detected, retrying once.")
   - `dist/selection-BxHKv2QU.js`: trajectory constant NON_DELIVERABLE_TERMINAL_TURN_REASON propagated
3. `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-trajectory-status` — 19/19 passed
4. `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution` — 207/207 passed (204 existing + 3 new)
5. `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier` — passed
6. `pnpm test src/trajectory/export` — passed
7. Combined test run of all 4 affected suites: Test Files 4 passed, Tests 260 passed

Evidence after fix:
- Clean build from empty dist directory compiles all changed files with zero errors
- New `NON_DELIVERABLE_TERMINAL_TURN_USER_TEXT` constant is present in the dist bundle
- Auto-retry counter `nonDeliverableTurnRetriesRemaining` and consumer `consumeNonDeliverableTurnRetry` compiled into the runtime bundle
- Trajectory `NON_DELIVERABLE_TERMINAL_TURN_REASON` constant flows through selection bundle
- 3 new unit tests verify: (a) auto-retry triggers with empty payloads → embedded runner called 2×; (b) exhausted retry returns kind: "final" with specific message; (c) fallback-exhausted skips retry but still surfaces specific message

Observed result after fix:
- 260 tests across 4 test files all pass with zero regressions
- Clean build (empty dist → full dist) succeeds with exit 0
- All 3 code layers (trajectory, failure-copy, reply-usage-state) verified present in built JS bundles
- When trajectoryTerminalError === "non_deliverable_terminal_turn" with empty payloads, the reply layer auto-retries once, then surfaces actionable user message

What was not tested: Full end-to-end reproduction with Telegram DM under heavy cacheRead load. Requires production-like environment with live API keys and sustained load to trigger the race between stop_reason=tool_use and turn closure. The built dist evidence and 260 passing tests cover the code paths at the unit and build-verification level.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
